title: 3 solo RPGs / 3 reviews / 3 play sessions (Rage in Hell, The Outcast, and Corny Gron) (solo RPG)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kYmLSudfROM

A triple feature with a twist: Daniel (@dungeondive) reviews three indie solo zine games by matching each to a musical genre. Rage in Hell (Justin Sirois) is grindcore and industrial — Bolt Thrower, Front 242 — a gonzo arena brawler about a stranded star warrior nano-forging weapons to fight through fifteen techno-demons. Its backstory (the Third Demon War, after the Vampire Freedom War and the 72nd Alien War) is pure 80s trash-cinema joy, and Daniel's run is gloriously brief: Slade Stackhouse fells an Iron Enchantress with a needle stinger, then gets pulped by a Sand Devil's Kraken in round two. The Outcast (César Kakule) is introspective cosmic jazz — Sun Ra, Pharoah Sanders — a journaling game of reputation and redemption. Daniel audibles mid-video into a new setting: clumsy young Brother Darius, blamed when his space-monastery veers toward an asteroid field, crawling into the ship's bowels to clear his name, shedding snags as his confidence grows. Chëdny Groń (with its expanded Beyond adventurer's guide) is pastoral prog and dark folk — early Genesis, Current 93 — a point-crawl descended from Dark Fort, with sub-dungeons like a lord's manor and a bear-guarded cave, plus a setting book whose sprawling bestiary Daniel compares to Vermis. That last one, he says, will see the most table time.

----

Daniel reviewed these games as musical genres — grindcore, cosmic jazz, dark folk. What genre is your favourite game, and which album would you shelve it next to?
